mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly growth versus 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals imminent labor market weakness, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive more than 55% of the time over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds inflation derived from the CPI index, real interest rates are restrictive, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal (real rate > 2%) fails to produce negative 20-day returns with a hit rate above 60% in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During early recovery from a recession, rising housing starts indicate strengthening construction activity and consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: early recovery period (first 24 months after recession trough)
failure_condition: In the early recovery regime, the signal (HOUST rising) yields positive 20-day returns less than 55% of the time over two recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In-sample Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change with 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A large monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls signals immediate labor market contraction, reducing aggregate demand and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 200,000 in one month
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: The joint trigger fails to produce positive 20-day returns with a hit rate above 55% in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, any decrease in the federal funds rate signals a dovish policy pivot that reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by at least 25 basis points in one month
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4% and a 25bp cut occurs, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive in less than 60% of cases over a 10-year walk-forward.
